changes.check: Stage 2A — Golden run corpus: fields present
changes.result: WARNING — architectural split Cursor must surface
changes.citation: backend/artifacts/golden_runs/ exists and is non-empty (30+ sprint-named directories). Golden run analysis_result.json top-level keys (sprint14_2_thyroid_immune_mini_fix_b): ['analysis_id', 'biomarkers', 'burden_hash', 'clusters', 'derived_markers', 'insights', 'meta', 'overall_score', 'primary_driver_system_id', 'replay_manifest', 'status', 'system_capacity_scores', 'unmapped_biomarkers']. Fields required by prompt fixture spec (consumer_domain_scores, clinician_report_v1, narrative_report_v1, interpretation_display_layer_v1, result_version) are NOT present in golden runs — these are assembled by build_analysis_result_dto() at retrieval time. Phase 2 fixture must be a snapshot of builder output, not a raw golden run file. Cursor must document this architectural split in Phase 1 current-state inventory before building the fixture.

changes.check: Stage 2B — Behavioural scope: Sentinel defect class promotion
changes.citation: Promoting persisted_result_replay from status_reporting to active_deterministic changes Sentinel guard behaviour. Adding 8 new defect classes changes what Sentinel reports and fails on. Each new class must point to an active deterministic test before the entry is added to escaped_defects_v1.json — no placeholder entries permitted (prompt line 593).

changes.check: Stage 2B — Behavioural scope: DTO compatibility guard
changes.citation: New DTO root-key compatibility tests must assert against build_analysis_result_dto() output (builders.py lines 60-87). If any of the 23 DTO root keys are absent from the fixture, the test must fail loudly. Fixture snapshot must be taken from a current known-good run — not hand-invented — to avoid builder drift.

frontend/e2e/** returns no files — no Playwright suite exists. Prompt explicitly permits backend API-to-DTO smoke test as minimum viable alternative (prompt lines 423-435). Cursor must NOT build Playwright infrastructure in this sprint.

persistence_service.py lines 165, 359, 597: result_version stored and retrieved. Defaults to '1.0.0' when absent. Golden runs lack this field — stale detection can key off version string comparison or absence. No forbidden-file changes required for stale detection logic in tests.

STOP conditions (prompt lines 344-352): no persisted fixture can be established; DTO cannot be loaded deterministically; stale state undetectable; frontend render requirements unmappable; replay requires broad DTO restructuring. All STOP conditions are deterministic and evaluable. Given DTO builder is readable and golden runs exist, none of these STOP conditions should trigger — but Cursor must explicitly verify each during Phase 1.

Forbidden files correctly exclude: backend/core/scoring/**, backend/ssot/*.yaml (scoring_policy, biomarkers, units), knowledge_bus/**, frontend/app/(app)/results/page.tsx, frontend/app/components/**, automation_bus control plane scripts. All fixture and test work stays within allowed paths (backend/tests/**, sentinel/packs/**, docs/audit-papers/). No path from authorised work to forbidden files detectable.

verdict: HARDENED. All five front matter fields valid. risk_level HIGH and change_type MIXED correctly classified. All mandatory authority paths confirmed present on disk. Critical architectural fact surfaced: golden run corpus stores raw analytics output — fields consumer_domain_scores, clinician_report_v1, narrative_report_v1, interpretation_display_layer_v1, result_version are assembled by build_analysis_result_dto() at retrieval time and are NOT in golden run files. Phase 2 persisted replay fixture must be a snapshot of builder output, not a raw golden run. Phase 1 test timestamp filter will SKIP all existing sprint-named golden runs — Cursor must address this in Phase 2. No Playwright suite exists — backend API-to-DTO smoke path is the correct minimum viable alternative. One warning: timestamp filter gap in Phase 1 test. Zero blockers. Cursor may proceed to Stage 3 kernel start.
